---
title: "RoomRadar"
subtitle: "Predicting Hotel Cancellations & Room Prices with Machine Learning"
author: "Klimentina Chapovska"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    footer: "RoomRadar — MADA Final Project"
    logo: ""
    highlight-style: github
    code-block-height: 400px
---

## The Problem

Hotels lose significant revenue every year to **last-minute cancellations**.

> A booking on the books is not the same as a guest at the door.

**Two questions every revenue manager needs answered:**

1. Will this booking cancel before arrival?
2. What is the right price for this room?

Answering them early allows targeted action — deposits, incentives, outreach — before it's too late.

---

## The Data

**Hotel Reservations Dataset** — 36,275 bookings from an Indian hotel group (2017–2018)

| | |
|---|---|
| Rows | 36,275 |
| Features | 19 |
| Missing values | 0 |
| Cancellation rate | **32.8%** |
| Date range | 2017 – 2018 |

**No missing values. No duplicates.** One moderate challenge: class imbalance (2.1:1 ratio of kept to cancelled).

---

## What the Data Tells Us

**Three signals dominate cancellation risk:**

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}
**Lead time**
Bookings 3+ months ahead cancel at >50% in some segments.
Spearman |r| = **0.42**
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**Special requests**
0 requests → 43% cancel rate.
3+ requests → **0% cancel rate.**
Spearman |r| = 0.26
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
**Repeat guests**
New guests cancel at 33.6%.
Repeat guests cancel at **1.7%** — a 20× difference.
:::

::::

---

## What the Data Tells Us (cont.)

**Booking channel matters enormously:**

| Market Segment | Cancellation Rate |
|---|---|
| Online | **36.5%** |
| Offline | 23.8% |
| Corporate | **10.9%** |
| Complementary | 0% |

Online bookings are the hotel's biggest source of revenue *and* cancellations. Corporate contracts are far stickier.

---

## Approach: Two ML Pipelines

```
Raw data (36,275 rows)
        │
        ▼
  Data cleaning ──► Drop 37 invalid dates
        │           Drop 545 zero-price rows (regression only)
        ▼
Feature engineering (inside sklearn Pipeline — no leakage)
  total_nights · log_lead_time · prior_cancel_rate · arrival_season · ...
        │
        ├──► Classification pipeline ──► Cancellation probability
        │
        └──► Regression pipeline    ──► Predicted room price
```

Both models use **XGBoost** tuned with 5-fold cross-validation (30 random search iterations).

---

## Cancellation Model Results

Stratified 60/20/20 split, `random_state=42`.

| Model | Test AUC | Test F1 |
|---|---|---|
| Dummy | 0.500 | 0.000 |
| Logistic Regression | 0.863 | 0.696 |
| Decision Tree | 0.876 | 0.795 |
| **XGBoost (tuned)** | **0.953** | **0.827** |

**Probability calibration** (isotonic regression) applied post-hoc:
Brier score 0.084 → **0.079**.

**Decision threshold** selected at **0.34** (F1-optimal on validation set, never on test).

---

## Risk Bands

Three actionable tiers derived from the calibrated probability:

| Band | Probability | % of bookings | Actual cancel rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Low | < 30% | 63% | 6.6% |
| Medium | 30–60% | 8% | 39.9% |
| High | ≥ 60% | 29% | **87.3%** |

The **High band** flags 29% of bookings and is correct 87% of the time — a very reliable signal for operations teams.

---

## Price Prediction Results

Zero-price rows excluded (n = 545). Raw target outperforms log1p for tuned XGBoost.

| Model | Test R² | Test RMSE |
|---|---|---|
| Dummy (mean) | −0.00 | 32.81 pu |
| Ridge | 0.50 | 23.18 pu |
| Decision Tree (d=12) | 0.61 | 20.58 pu |
| **XGBoost (tuned)** | **0.755** | **16.24 pu** |

The model explains **75.5% of price variance** and is off by ~10 price units on average.

Top price drivers: room type, party size, market segment, arrival season, meal plan.

---

## The Live Application

**RoomRadar** — deployed React + FastAPI app

🔗 [roomradar-henna.vercel.app](https://roomradar-henna.vercel.app)

**Three sections:**

- **Predict** — enter booking details, receive calibrated cancellation probability, risk band, predicted price, and business recommendations
- **Dashboard** — interactive EDA charts: segment trends, seasonality, lead-time risk, price distributions
- **Performance** — model leaderboards, ROC curves, confusion matrices, feature importances

---

## Limitations

- **Data vintage** — 2017–2018 data from a single (unidentified) hotel. Results may not transfer to other properties or time periods.
- **Currency** — prices are in unspecified units; the dataset uses "$" notation but currency is unverified.
- **Modest overfitting** — train→val F1 gap of 0.10 for the cancellation model. Val→test gap is 0.005, confirming the gap is in train fit, not generalisation.
- **Unexplained variance** — R² 0.755 leaves 24.5% of price variance unexplained (promotions, loyalty pricing, competitor rates not in data).
- **No fairness analysis** — protected attributes were not examined. Historical patterns may encode past management decisions.

---

## Recommendations

**For the hotel:**

1. **Long lead-time + online channel** → require partial deposit or non-refundable rate
2. **Encourage special requests at booking** — guests who customise are 43% less likely to cancel
3. **Invest in repeat-guest programmes** — 20× lower cancellation rate; strong ROI case
4. **Flag High-band bookings 2–4 weeks before arrival** for proactive outreach

**For the model:**

- Retrain annually with fresh data
- Validate on the property's own booking history before operational use
- Consider fairness audit before any guest-facing deployment

---

## Summary

| | |
|---|---|
| Dataset | 36,275 hotel bookings, 19 features, no missing values |
| Cancellation AUC | **0.953** (XGBoost + isotonic calibration) |
| Price R² | **0.755** (XGBoost on raw target) |
| Strongest signal | Lead time (|r| = 0.42) |
| Biggest actionable gap | Online vs Corporate cancel rate (36.5% vs 10.9%) |
| Live app | [roomradar-henna.vercel.app](https://roomradar-henna.vercel.app) |

> With a calibrated probability and a risk band on every booking, revenue managers can act early — before cancellations happen.
